title: chamaecrista nictitans var. ramosa
intro: Chamaecrista nictitans var. ramosa, also known as partridge pea, is a perennial legume plant native to the southeastern United States. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Chamaecrista nictitans var. ramosa include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the dried leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
